title: Solo Piano Summit
subTitle: Stuttgart's Jazz Open Festival features Jacky Terrasson, Junior Mance, Geoff Keezer, Bill Charlap and Alex Wilson.

title: Munich Philharmonic Hall: Michel Camilo Quintet
subTitle: Broadcast from 1991 Münchner Klaviersommer, when Camilo was playing with his amazing Quintet.

title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Chucho Valdes and his Afro-Cuban messengers are the prime example of Latin-inspired jazz music; during his performance the pianist brings the streets of Havana to the Jazz a Vienne stage.

title: Fine and Mellow
subTitle: This compilation features recordings from the 1950s of Billie Holiday singing a string of her successes; enjoy this unique footage of one of jazz's all-time greats!

title: Jazz Samba: Baden Powell, Ernesto Ribeiro
subTitle: Recorded in 1971 in Paris, Brazilian, classically trained bossa nova guitarist Baden Powell and his quartet play various compositions, including some originals.

title: Christian McBride Quartet at Malta 2018
subTitle: Double bassist Christian McBride presents his band New Jawn at the Malta Jazz Festival on July 20, 2018; hailing from Philadelphia, this musician combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical.

title: Marcus Miller: Thoughts on Miles
subTitle: In the 2009 portrait of Marcus Miller, Miller looks back on his relationship with Miles Davis; he shares stories of the recording process of the album "Tutu," explaining how he wrote the title track.

title: Thelonious Monk: Jazz Greats
subTitle: The idiosyncratic pianist and composer Thelonious Monk in a performance with his quartet in Copenhagen, Denmark on April 17, 1966 and Warsaw, Poland on April 4, 1966.

subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosts the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

subTitle: Nubiyan Twist, a London-based band that operates on the intersection of soul and jazz, performs at the International Music Meeting Festival 2019.

subTitle: Two 1963 concerts featuring one of the most celebrated sextets in jazz history.

title: Lalah Hathaway
subTitle: Winner of five Grammys in four consecutive years, including the Grammy for best R&B album in 2017, Lalah Hathaway is a force impossible to ignore; see it transform the 2018 Sing Jazz festival scene into a real R&B club.

title: Newport Jazz Festival 1968
subTitle: In 1968, jazz's greatest artists appeared at the third Newport Jazz Festival in Rotterdam. This broadcast features the bands of pianist Dave Brubeck, vibraphonists Gary Burton and Red Norvo, and trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie.

subTitle: "The Skymasters" perform live with Clark Terry at the North Sea Jazz Festival.

subTitle: Selection of performances by some influential male jazz and pop vocalists showcases these legends' unique essence.

title: Al McKay -- Earth, Wind & Fire Experience
subTitle: The Earth, Wind & Fire experience needs little introduction: in this concert, Earth, Wind & Fire guitarist, writer and musical director Al McKay performs the band's disco and soul, keeping hits such as Boogie Wonderland and September alive.

title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

title: Blicher Hemmer Gadd: A Music Documentary
subTitle: Award-winning saxophonist Michael Blicher, Hammond player Dan Hemmer and drummer Steve Gadd celebrate their shared love for Hammond-organ soul jazz; the band's third album features vocalist Paul Carrack on ballad "The First One To Know."

subTitle: In 1987, prominent guitarist George Benson brought his band to kick off day three of the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; joining him to play songs from their joint album Collaborations was acoustic guitarist Earl Klugh.

title: Quincy Jones: 'Q' conducting his 'Dream Band'
subTitle: Jazz Icons: Quincy Jones'spotlights a young 'Q' conducting his 'dream band'-an 18-piece orchestra of world-renowned players such as Clark Terry, Phil Woods, Sahib Shihab, Budd Johnson and Benny Bailey. This recording features 5 songs and is one of the finest examples of big band jazz ever to be captured on film. Shot in Belgium in 1961, this concert is one of the only known visual documents of this legendary tour.

title: Long Tall Dexter Live in '64
subTitle: Featuring three concerts filmed in Holland, Switzerland, and Belgium that highlight the bebop musician's classic style and silky tone.

subTitle: In 1964 Louis Armstrong recorded his biggest-selling record, Hello, Dolly! He made assorted television appearances, especially in the 1950s and 1960s - the recorded film was a TV Show in Australia when Armstrong was at the peak of his career.

subTitle: As part of the Blue Note Jazz Festival and Blue Note Records' 75th anniversary, pianists Robert Glasper and Jason Moran deliver a jazz piano duet, resulting in an exploration of the history of jazz.

title: Bitches Brew Beyond
subTitle: Trumpeter Wallace Roney revives Miles Davis' legendary album, Bitches Brew. In this project - a brainchild of Miles' family - Miles' nephew takes part. This concert at Vienne's ancient theater in France is a grand tribute to Miles.

subTitle: Jaco Pastorius, a true musical innovator, takes the stage at the Montreal Jazz Festival; he is transforming the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument, wowing the audience in Canada with his unique style and talent.

title: Rhoda Scott & La Velle: Jazz à Vienne
subTitle: Although American soul and jazz organist Rhoda Scott spent most of her career in France, this performance at the antique theatre of Vienne is special: she presents an intimate and beautiful performance with US gospel and blues singer La Velle.

title: Hackensack
subTitle: Explore the music of John Coltrane; immerse yourself in the collection of performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's artistry.

title: The Maynard Ferguson Big Band
subTitle: The Maynard Ferguson Big Band's Winter Tour of 1980 included a stop at the Brussels Jazz Club; Ferguson and his band perform some hits of the day.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1982, American jazz fusion band Spyro Gyra took the stage at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; having struck gold with their album "Morning Dance" just a few years prior, they played a slick set with exuberant energy.
